title: 「Somewhere / Nowhere / Elsewhere」句型
titleEn: Using somewhere, nowhere and elsewhere for place
formula: Subject + verb + somewhere / nowhere / elsewhere + other information

formulas.formula: somewhere / nowhere + adjective / to + base verb

example: The missing book must be somewhere in this room.
exampleZh: 遺失的書一定在這個房間的某處。

examples.en: The missing book must be somewhere in this room.
examples.zh: 遺失的書一定在這個房間的某處。

meaning.zh: Somewhere 表示地點存在但不明或不指定；nowhere 表示完全沒有地方；elsewhere 表示另一個不同的地方。

rules.en: Somewhere means a place exists but its location is unknown or unimportant.
rules.zh: Somewhere 表示地點存在，但位置不明或不重要。
examples: I left my phone somewhere in the office.

rules.en: Nowhere already carries a negative meaning.
rules.zh: Nowhere 本身已帶有否定意思，不要再加入 not 造成雙重否定。
Elsewhere 表示與目前地方不同的另一處。
examples: The service may be available elsewhere.

rules.en: Do not normally put in, at or to directly before these place adverbs.
rules.zh: 表示位置或目的地時，通常不用 in、at 或 to 直接放在這些地方副詞前。

rules.en: Put an adjective after somewhere or nowhere.
rules.zh: 形容詞通常放在 somewhere 或 nowhere 後面。

rules.en: These words may also express abstract direction.
rules.zh: 這些字亦可表達答案、立場或證據方向等抽象意思。

benefits.en: It distinguishes unknown, unavailable and alternative places clearly.
benefits.zh: 它能清楚分辨位置不明、沒有地方和另一處。
它能把「某個不知名地方」等較長說法縮短。

benefits.en: It helps describe available places and choices.
benefits.zh: 它方便表達有沒有地方可坐、溫習或留宿。

benefits.en: It supports both physical and abstract meanings.
benefits.zh: 它既可描述實際地點，亦可表達答案或證據的方向。

benefits.en: It makes place descriptions more natural.
benefits.zh: 它能減少重複使用 place，使地點描述更自然。

en: Rewrite each sentence using somewhere, nowhere or elsewhere.
Preserve the original meaning and avoid double negatives.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 使用 somewhere、nowhere 或 elsewhere 改寫以下句子。
保留原句意思，並避免雙重否定。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。

questions.prompt: I left my keys in a place, but I cannot remember where it was.
questions.promptZh: 我把鎖匙放在一個地方，但我記不起那是甚麼地方。
questions.answer: I left my keys somewhere, but I cannot remember where.
questions.answerZh: 我把鎖匙放在某處，但我記不起在哪裏。
questions.highlight: I left my keys somewhere, but I cannot remember where.

questions.prompt: We have no place to sit.
questions.promptZh: 我們沒有地方坐。
questions.answer: We have nowhere to sit.
questions.answerZh: 我們無處可坐。
questions.highlight: We have nowhere to sit.

questions.prompt: This café is too crowded, so let us eat in another place.
questions.promptZh: 這間咖啡店太擠迫，所以我們到另一個地方吃東西吧。
questions.answer: This café is too crowded, so let us eat elsewhere.

questions.highlight: Mia wants to live somewhere quiet near the sea, but she has not chosen a location yet.

questions.prompt: When reliable data points in a different direction, we should not force the answer to remain where we first expected it.
